title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
subtitle: Multi-Agent Orchestration
description: Coordinates how multiple specialized agents execute tasks in sequence or parallel, distributes work, and aggregates results. The core challenge is balancing flexibility with controllability across distributed agent execution.
sub_problems: Task decomposition: Breaking complex tasks into parallelizable subtasks
Execution orchestration: DAG scheduling, sequential/parallel/conditional branching
State synchronization: Sharing context and intermediate results across agents
Result aggregation: Merging multiple agent outputs and resolving conflicts
Dynamic routing: Selecting appropriate agents based on task type at runtime
Dual pipeline reuse: Supporting multiple orchestration pipelines of different lengths while sharing common stages
Memory cleanup after writes: Preventing token explosion in long execution loops while retaining necessary context
Completeness assessment: Auto-detecting LLM output truncation and triggering adaptive retries
Pattern selection: Choosing different orchestration modes for different task types within the same system
Iteration convergence: Determining when agent loops should terminate (should_stop signal design)
Dynamic queue expansion: Adding new topics to the scheduling queue when discovered during processing
SOP encoding: Translating human team standard operating procedures into agent message subscription relationships
Message bus routing: Environment routing messages to target roles based on send_to/cause_by fields
Dual-mode switching: Toggling between fixed SOP pipelines and dynamic TeamLeader routing within the same team
Budget circuit breaker: Implementing global cost control across parallel agent execution
Recursion protection: Preventing child agents from infinitely spawning new child agents
Result naturalization: Converting child agent outputs into user-facing natural language responses
best_practices: Start simple: Single agent with multiple tools often easier to debug than multiple agents
Clear role boundaries: Each agent's responsibilities must be distinct to avoid overlap
Serializable state: Agent-exchanged state must be serializable for persistence and recovery
Timeouts and circuit breakers: Parallel agents need timeout mechanisms to prevent single-agent blocking
Result validation: Check consistency when aggregating results and handle conflicts
MCP Server binding for tool isolation: Each agent sees only its required tools, reducing tool selection interference
Fan-in/fan-out for independent analysis: Parallel execution of concept and algorithm analysis saves ~50% time when dependencies don't exist
Analysis loop detection: Track last N tool calls; inject write operation correction when all are read operations
Federated orchestration: Different modules can use different orchestration modes without forced uniformity
Lazy initialization: Defer non-essential agent creation until first use to reduce resource waste
Async wrapper: Provide async wrappers for non-thread-safe components to support smooth parallel mode transitions
Message routing decoupling: Roles communicate through Environment message bus rather than direct calls
Idle detection with three conditions: msg_buffer empty AND no todo AND no news before marking idle
TeamLeader pause-and-wait: Set state to -1 after task assignment to pause self, resume after member feedback
Tool whitelist over blacklist: Child agents register only explicitly granted tools for better security
Result passback via message bus: Avoid child agents holding parent agent references to reduce coupling

values.MetaGPT: Dual-mode: SOP implicit DAG (_watch subscription) + MGX TeamLeader dynamic routing
values.Nanobot: Spawn-and-forget: Main agent asynchronously spawns child agents via spawn tool

values.MiroThinker: Sequential execution (child agents in independent rounds)
values.DeepCode: mcp_agent ParallelLLM primitive with fan-out parallelism + fan-in aggregation
values.DeepTutor: Research module Semaphore-controlled parallelism, asyncio.gather batch execution
values.MetaGPT: asyncio.gather parallel execution of all non-idle roles, no concurrency limit
values.Nanobot: Native parallelism: asyncio.create_task supports multiple simultaneous child agents

values.MiroThinker: Independent message_history prevents main agent pollution
values.DeepCode: Function parameter passing dir_info Dict + ConciseMemoryAgent write-then-clear
values.DeepTutor: Three-layer memory persistence + GuidedSession session state machine
values.Nanobot: Complete isolation: Child agents have independent messages list, no main agent context sharing

values.DeepCode: ParallelLLM internal management, no explicit concurrency config
values.DeepTutor: asyncio.Semaphore(max_parallel) + asyncio.Lock for shared state protection
values.MetaGPT: No built-in semaphore, relies on n_round iteration limit and NoMoneyException budget circuit breaker
values.Nanobot: No explicit limit: _running_tasks dict tracks but doesn't limit count

values.GPT-Researcher: Each sub-query gets independent GPTResearcher instance
values.MiroThinker: YAML config defines independent tool set per sub_agent
values.DeepCode: Each agent binds independent MCP Server via server_names
values.DeepTutor: Research unified _call_tool routing, Solve isolates via ToolAgent
values.MetaGPT: Each role has independent tool_execution_map, TeamLeader exposes via @register_tool
values.Nanobot: Whitelist registration: Child agents explicitly exclude message/spawn tools to prevent recursion

values.DeepCode: 7 specialist agents independent, chained via async functions
values.DeepTutor: 5 independent orchestrators each choose best-fit orchestration mode
values.MetaGPT: Roles autonomously observe→think→act, Environment only routes messages without decision interference
values.Nanobot: High autonomy: Child agents run 15 independent loops, return results via MessageBus

values.DeepCode: Agents use async with context manager for on-demand init and cleanup
values.DeepTutor: Solve loop agents created only on first use, reducing startup overhead
values.Nanobot: On-demand creation: Each spawn creates new ToolRegistry and messages

values.DeepCode: Fan-in agent auto-receives all fan-out results, pipeline passes via return values
values.Nanobot: MessageBus injection: Results as system messages trigger main agent natural response

values.DeepCode: Paper-to-Code (8-stage) and Chat-to-Code (4-stage) share implementation layer
values.MetaGPT: Classic SOP (use_fixed_sop) and MGX dynamic routing (use_mgx) coexist in same team

values.DeepCode: ConciseMemoryAgent clears history after each write_file, retains only plan
values.MetaGPT: RoleZeroLongTermMemory supports vector retrieval, memory_k limits short-term history

solutions.title: Lead Agent → SubagentExecutor dynamic delegation with task_tool.py parallel orchestration
solutions.description: Lead Agent delegates subtasks to Subagents via task() tool in task_tool.py. SubagentExecutor (executor.py) uses dual thread pool architecture: _scheduler_pool(3) for scheduling + _execution_pool(3) for execution. SubagentConfig (config.py) defines name/tools/model/timeout, supporting model="inherit" to reuse parent agent model. SubagentLimitMiddleware truncates task calls exceeding MAX_CONCURRENT_SUBAGENTS=3. Tool isolation via disallowed_tools=["task"] prevents recursive nesting.
design_philosophy: Dynamic orchestrator: Lead Agent decides parallelization strategy at runtime based on task nature, not compile-time fixed graph
Dual thread pool isolation: _scheduler_pool schedules, _execution_pool executes, no mutual blocking
Tool as delegation: task() tool is delegation entry point, Subagents disable task tool to prevent recursion
Model inheritance: model="inherit" reuses parent agent model, reducing config redundancy
migration_scenarios: Complex tasks requiring runtime-determined parallelization strategy
Multiple subagents executing in parallel with timeout protection and state tracking
Existing LangGraph DAG projects needing parallel delegation within nodes

solutions.title: DeepResearchSkill tree-based recursion breadth=4 depth=2 with asyncio.Semaphore(2) concurrency control
solutions.description: DeepResearchSkill in gpt_researcher/skills/deep_research.py implements tree-based recursive research: each layer generates breadth sub-queries, each creating independent GPTResearcher instance executing conduct_research(). Breadth halves per layer via max(2, breadth//2) to control exponential explosion. asyncio.Semaphore(2) limits concurrency. Cross-layer sharing of visited_urls for deduplication + learnings for knowledge accumulation. Default parameters breadth=4 depth=2 produce ~36 searches + 20 LLM calls, taking 3-5 minutes.
design_philosophy: Independent researcher per sub-query: GPTResearcher(query=sub_q) isolates state preventing pollution
Breadth halving per layer: max(2, breadth//2) controls exponential explosion
Cross-layer knowledge accumulation: learnings passed to subsequent layers for informed sub-query generation
Concurrency control: asyncio.Semaphore(2) prevents API rate limiting
migration_scenarios: Search-intensive agents requiring tree-based deep research
Multi-source information aggregation needing parallel sub-queries
Research agents needing search explosion control

solutions.title: run_sub_agent() child agents as tools with expose_sub_agents_as_tools() config-driven approach
solutions.description: run_sub_agent() in orchestrator.py:327-499 executes child agents independently: independent system_prompt, tool_definitions, message_history, max_turns limit. expose_sub_agents_as_tools() in settings.py exposes child agents as callable tools for main agent. YAML config (conf/agent/multi_agent.yaml) defines main_agent and sub_agents (agent-browsing/agent-research/agent-citation), each with independent tool set and turn limits.
design_philosophy: Child agents as tools: Main agent delegates subtasks via unified tool interface
Independent context: Each child agent has isolated message history preventing main agent pollution
YAML config-driven: sub_agents config defines tool set and turn limits
Independent session tracking: Child agent execution recorded in isolated session
migration_scenarios: Multi-role scenarios needing child agent context and tool set isolation
Projects where main agent dynamically delegates subtasks
Sequential workflows needing role isolation without parallelism

solutions.title: 7-Agent orchestration engine
solutions.description: DeepCode uses mcp_agent framework's ParallelLLM for fan-in/fan-out parallelism, 7 specialist agents with MCP Server tool isolation, supporting Paper-to-Code and Chat-to-Code dual pipeline modes.
design_philosophy: Specialist agent separation: Each agent handles specific domain (analysis, implementation, testing)
MCP Server tool isolation: Each agent accesses only domain-relevant tools
Dual pipeline architecture: Paper-to-Code (8-stage) and Chat-to-Code (4-stage) share implementation layer
Fan-in/fan-out parallelism: Independent analysis tasks execute in parallel, results aggregated
migration_scenarios: Code generation projects needing specialist agent separation
Multi-stage processing requiring both sequential and parallel execution
Projects supporting multiple input modalities (paper vs chat)

solutions.title: Multi-module dual-loop orchestration
solutions.description: Unified BaseAgent abstraction layer: all agents inherit from BaseAgent in src/agents/base_agent.py:35, providing unified LLM calls, token tracking, prompt loading, config management. Five independent orchestrators (Research/Solve/Verify/Refine/Explain) each select best-fit mode: Dual-Loop for iterative refinement, DynamicQueue for topic discovery, Pipeline for sequential stages, StateMachine for state-driven transitions.
design_philosophy: Unified abstraction: BaseAgent provides common interface for all agent types
Federated mode selection: Each module autonomously chooses orchestration mode
Three-layer memory: Persistent storage + session state + short-term context
Guided session state machine: Tracks conversation state across multiple turns
migration_scenarios: Educational systems needing multi-stage tutoring workflows
Projects with heterogeneous task types requiring different orchestration modes
Systems needing persistent learning state across sessions

solutions.title: SOP-driven multi-role orchestration
solutions.description: MetaGPT uses SOP implicit DAG (_watch subscription) + MGXEnv TeamLeader dynamic routing for dual-mode multi-role orchestration, asyncio.gather for parallel execution. RoleContext encapsulates memory/msg_buffer/state/todo with Pydantic serialization. Roles autonomously observe→think→act; Environment handles message routing without decision interference.
design_philosophy: SOP as implicit DAG: _watch subscriptions define dependencies without explicit graph
Dual-mode flexibility: Fixed SOP for predictable workflows, TeamLeader for dynamic routing
Role autonomy: Each role independently decides actions based on observations
Message bus decoupling: Roles communicate through Environment, not direct calls
migration_scenarios: Software development workflows with defined SOP
Projects needing both fixed pipelines and dynamic task routing
Multi-role systems requiring autonomous agent coordination

solutions.title: Spawn child agent async orchestration
solutions.description: asyncio.create_task async spawning: main agent creates background asyncio Task via spawn tool, returns confirmation immediately without blocking main loop (subagent.py:81-84). Independent tool registry: each child agent has isolated ToolRegistry. Child agents run 15 independent loops, return results via MessageBus. Whitelist tool registration prevents recursive spawning.
design_philosophy: Fire-and-forget spawning: Main agent continues immediately after spawn, child runs asynchronously
Complete isolation: Child agents have independent messages, tools, and execution context
Result injection: Child results injected as system messages triggering main agent response
Recursion prevention: Whitelist registration excludes spawn/message tools from child agents
migration_scenarios: Systems needing non-blocking child agent spawning
Projects with highly independent subtasks suitable for async execution
Scenarios where child agent completion doesn't block main agent progress

solutions.title: Lifecycle subagent registry
solutions.description: SubagentRunRecord registry: in-memory Map + disk persistence tracking complete state of each child agent execution (src/agents/subagent-registry.ts:42). Depth-limit recursion protection via maxSpawnDepth configuration. Lifecycle hooks for agent creation/execution/completion. State serialization for recovery and audit.
design_philosophy: Persistent registry: All subagent executions tracked in memory and disk
Depth-limited recursion: maxSpawnDepth prevents infinite spawn chains
Lifecycle management: Hooks for creation/execution/completion events
State recovery: Serialized state enables resumption after failures
migration_scenarios: Systems requiring audit trail of all agent executions
Projects needing recursion depth limits for safety
Applications requiring agent state persistence and recovery

solutions.title: PlanningFlow plan-driven orchestration
solutions.description: PlanningFlow as orchestration core: not a DAG but linear plan executor. LLM generates step list first, then executes sequentially (app/flow/planning.py:94-134). Each step can spawn subtasks or delegate to specialized agents. Plan refinement based on execution feedback. Supports plan branching for conditional logic.
design_philosophy: Plan-first execution: LLM generates complete plan before execution begins
Linear execution model: Steps execute sequentially with feedback loops
Adaptive refinement: Plan adjusts based on step execution results
Conditional branching: Plan supports if/else logic for dynamic paths
migration_scenarios: Complex workflows benefiting from upfront planning
Systems where execution plan visibility is important
Projects needing plan refinement based on intermediate results
